Issue #1 anurdin01

Issue #1: Research Claude AI

Author: anurdin01 | Updated: 2026-05-30T04:26:40Z

Description

Research how Claude AI powers the report-management workflow in this repo (smoke-test reports generated per project and published to GitHub Pages). The goal is to document what Claude AI is, how it is used to produce and manage reports, and the practices that keep it reliable and cost-effective.

Scope

  • Efficient token use — context management, prompt caching, scoping reads to only the needed files/lines, and trimming output so report runs stay cheap and fast.
  • Prompting — how report tasks are phrased, system/CLAUDE.md guidance, structured output, and repeatable prompt patterns for consistent report formatting.
  • Connectors (MCP) — external data/tooling Claude reaches through MCP servers (e.g. Microsoft Learn docs) and how connectors feed report content.
  • GitHub integration — issues as task tracking, the gh CLI, and publishing reports to GitHub Pages.
  • Other — models (Opus / Sonnet / Haiku), safety/permissions, and limits/quotas relevant to report runs.

Deliverable

A short written summary (with the screenshots below) describing Claude AI in the context of report management.

Result :

Issue #2 anurdin01

Issue #2: Research Brainstorming Used Claude AI

Author: anurdin01 | Updated: 2026-05-30T04:26:42Z

Description

Research how brainstorming with Claude AI is used to plan and shape report management. The goal is to document how brainstorming (the brainstorming skill / interactive design exploration) is applied before building report features, and how that improves the reports produced in this repo.

Scope

  • Efficient token use — keeping brainstorming sessions focused so exploration does not waste context; capturing decisions instead of re-deriving them.
  • Prompting — how requirements and design intent are drawn out before implementation, and how those prompts translate into report structure and content.
  • Connectors (MCP) — pulling reference material (e.g. Microsoft Learn docs) into brainstorming to ground ideas in real constraints.
  • GitHub integration — turning brainstorm outcomes into issues/tasks and tracking them through to published reports.
  • Other — when to brainstorm vs. implement directly, and how brainstorming reduces rework on report formats.

Deliverable

A short written summary (with the screenshots below) describing the brainstorming workflow with Claude AI for report management.

Result :

Issue #3 anurdin01

Issue #3: Research Superpower Used Claude AI

Author: anurdin01 | Updated: 2026-05-30T04:26:44Z

Description

Research the "superpowers" (skills / agents) of Claude AI and how they accelerate report management. The goal is to document the reusable skills and sub-agents available (slash-command skills, MCP-backed skills, specialized agents) and how each contributes to generating and maintaining reports in this repo.

Scope

  • Efficient token use — how skills and sub-agents isolate work so the main context stays small (delegating searches/reviews and keeping only conclusions).
  • Prompting — how skills package proven prompt patterns (e.g. brainstorming, code-review, deep-research) so report tasks are repeatable and consistent.
  • Connectors (MCP) — MCP-backed skills (e.g. Microsoft Learn docs) that extend Claude with external knowledge during report work.
  • GitHub integration — skills/agents that drive gh, review PRs/diffs, and publish report output.
  • Other — catalog the relevant skills/agents, when to use each, and their limits.

Deliverable

A short written summary cataloging the Claude AI skills/agents ("superpowers") applied to report management, with supporting screenshots.

Result :

To be added.

Issue #4 anurdin01

Issue #4: Research Report Use CLI

Author: anurdin01 | Updated: 2026-05-30T04:26:46Z

Description

Research how reports are generated and managed using the CLI (Claude Code in the terminal). The goal is to document the end-to-end flow that produces the per-project smoke-test reports in this repo and publishes them to GitHub Pages.

Scope

  • Efficient token use — running report generation in the CLI with minimal context, scoped file reads, and caching so each run is fast and cheap.
  • Prompting — the commands/prompts and CLAUDE.md guidance that drive a report run to a consistent, repeatable format.
  • Connectors (MCP) — any MCP servers/data sources the CLI uses while assembling report content.
  • GitHub integration — using gh for issues, committing report output, and publishing to GitHub Pages (the URLs below are the published results).
  • Other — folder/date/run naming conventions (<project>/<date>/<run>/index.html) and how runs are organized across projects.

Deliverable

A short written summary of the CLI report-generation workflow, linking the published reports below.

Result :

  • https://gothrumedia.github.io/reports/gothru-dashboard/2026-05-30/public-smoke-11/index.html
  • https://gothrumedia.github.io/reports/gothru-photo-studio/2026-05-30/public-smoke/index.html
  • https://gothrumedia.github.io/reports/panorra-360-mobile/2026-05-30/public-smoke/index.html
  • https://gothrumedia.github.io/reports/panorra-360-pwa/2026-05-29/public-smoke/index.html
